title: strigosella circinata
intro: Strigosella circinata, also known as the Hooked Calabar Nut, is a plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to induce vomiting and act as a purgative. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Strigosella circinata include infusions and decoctions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
